edges.description: Shared genetic liability is represented upstream of altered cognitive, reward, and appetite-control circuitry.
Genetic and behavioral vulnerability is represented upstream of the sustained restriction that produces starvation-adaptation biology.
Circuit-level dysregulation is modeled upstream of restrictive eating and persistent food-choice behavior.
Persistent restriction contributes to low body weight and weight loss.
edges.target: Low Body Weight and Weight Loss

edges.description: Starvation-associated endocrine and metabolic changes are modeled upstream of systemic immune and microbiome-associated alterations.
Starvation physiology contributes to downstream cardiovascular complications requiring clinical monitoring.
Cytokine and microbiome findings are associated with depressive symptoms in adolescent AN cohorts.
Cardiovascular monitoring addresses bradycardia and other complications of AN.

nodes.description: Restrictive eating and food avoidance are core behavioral manifestations of AN.

evidence.reference_title: A Feasibility Trial of Theta Burst Stimulation in Anorexia Nervosa (AN)
evidence.snippet: Anorexia Nervosa (AN) is a life-threatening eating disorder characterised by an intense fear of weight gain and disturbed body image, which motivates severe dietary restriction or other weight loss behaviours (e.g. purging).

nodes.description: Altered brain networks involved in appetite, valuation, cognitive control, and restrictive food choice are represented as circuit-level mechanisms contributing to persistent restrictive eating.

evidence.reference_title: Deciphering the Neural Mechanisms of Restrictive Eating in Anorexia Nervosa Using Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation
evidence.snippet: This study will examine the impact of high-frequency repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation on food choice behavior and related neural activity.

nodes.description: Bradycardia is a clinically important cardiovascular sign in AN and is part of the broader cardiovascular monitoring burden.

evidence.reference_title: Determinants of severe bradycardia in adolescents hospitalized for anorexia nervosa.
evidence.snippet: Forty-eight percent of the AN patient admissions were due to severe

eating disorders may cause electrolyte
abnormalities, bradycardia, disturbances in reproductive hormones, and decreased
bone density

nodes.description: AN is associated with increased risk of major adverse cardiovascular events and other cardiovascular conditions, with some risks concentrated early after diagnosis and ischemic heart disease risk emerging later.

evidence.reference_title: Incidence and Risk of Cardiovascular Outcomes in Patients With Anorexia Nervosa
evidence.snippet: The study population included 2081 patients with AN and 20 810 matched controls, for a total of 22 891 participants (mean [SD] age, 24.9 [9.9] years; 91.3% female).

evidence.reference_title: Incidence and Risk of Cardiovascular Outcomes in Patients With Anorexia Nervosa
evidence.snippet: Compared with the control group, the AN group had significantly higher risks of MACE (adjusted HR [AHR], 3.78; 95% CI, 2.83-5.05) and any cardiovascular condition (AHR, 1.93; 95% CI, 1.54-2.41).

nodes.description: Cognitive behavioral therapy is a core psychotherapy for anorexia nervosa and is included among treatments of choice.

evidence.snippet: The treatment of choice for AN includes cognitive behavioral therapy and family-based therapy for children and adolescents.

nodes.description: Depression is a common psychiatric comorbidity in AN.

evidence.snippet: It is frequently associated with other psychiatric disorders, such as depression, anxiety and obsessive-compulsive disorders as well as numerous physical complications.

disorder have high lifetime rates of depression (76.3% for bulimia nervosa,
65.5% for binge-eating disorder, and 49.5% for anorexia nervosa)

nodes.description: Family-based therapy is a treatment of choice for children and adolescents with anorexia nervosa.

evidence.snippet: The treatment of choice for AN includes cognitive behavioral therapy and family-based therapy for children and adolescents.

nodes.description: Adolescent AN is associated with non-uniform cytokine alterations and microbiome associations that vary across acute illness, weight recovery, and follow-up.

evidence.reference_title: Cytokine and Microbiome Changes in Adolescents with Anorexia Nervosa at Admission, Discharge, and One-Year Follow-Up
evidence.snippet: Relative to the HC group, serum levels of IL-1β and IL-6 were significantly lower during the acute phase (admission) of AN.

evidence.reference_title: Cytokine and Microbiome Changes in Adolescents with Anorexia Nervosa at Admission, Discharge, and One-Year Follow-Up
evidence.snippet: We found associations between cytokines and bodyweight, illness duration, depressive symptoms, and the microbiome.

nodes.description: Restriction produces low body weight and may present clinically as weight loss or failure to maintain expected weight.
nodes.id: Low Body Weight and Weight Loss

evidence.reference_title: A Feasibility Trial of Theta Burst Stimulation in Anorexia Nervosa (AN)
evidence.snippet: Anorexia Nervosa (AN) is a life-threatening eating disorder characterised by an intense fear of weight gain and disturbed body image, which motivates severe dietary restriction or other weight loss behaviours (e.g. purging).

nodes.description: Olanzapine has moderate evidence for supporting weight gain in anorexia nervosa, while psychopharmacotherapy otherwise lacks clear efficacy.

evidence.snippet: With the exception of moderate evidence supporting the use of olanzapine regarding weight gain, there is currently no evidence for the efficacy of psychopharmacotherapy in AN.

nodes.description: Anorexia nervosa is modeled as a heritable, polygenic disorder with shared genetic architecture across psychiatric traits and pathways that plausibly influence metabolic and neurobehavioral vulnerability.

evidence.reference_title: Genome-wide analysis of anorexia nervosa and major psychiatric disorders and related traits reveals genetic overlap and identifies novel risk loci for anorexia nervosa
evidence.snippet: Anorexia nervosa (AN) is a heritable eating disorder (50–60%) with an array of commonly comorbid psychiatric disorders and related traits.

evidence.reference_title: Genome-wide analysis of anorexia nervosa and major psychiatric disorders and related traits reveals genetic overlap and identifies novel risk loci for anorexia nervosa
evidence.snippet: Using conditional FDR we identified 58 novel AN loci.

nodes.description: Sustained energy restriction produces peripheral endocrine and metabolic changes involving appetite hormones, growth-axis markers, glucose, insulin, thyroid hormone, sex hormones, and leptin.

evidence.reference_title: Peripheral Biomarkers of Anorexia Nervosa: A Meta-Analysis
evidence.snippet: acylated ghrelin, adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH), carboxy-terminal collagen crosslinks (CTX), cholesterol, cortisol, des-acyl ghrelin, ghrelin, growth hormone (GH), obestatin, and soluble leptin receptor levels were significantly higher in cases of AN compared with those in non-AN controls.

evidence.reference_title: Peripheral Biomarkers of Anorexia Nervosa: A Meta-Analysis
evidence.snippet: Our findings indicate that peripheral biomarkers may be linked to the pathophysiology of AN, such as processes of adaptation to starvation.
